What will you do if the plan fails?
Sandra, what will you do this weekend if the weather is nice?
If the weather is nice, I will have a dinner in the garden. Otherwise, we will stay inside.
Smart. And what is your backup for the food, in case someone does not eat meat?
In case someone does not eat meat, I will prepare a vegetable dish too. I think about every situation.
You plan everything. Will it really go well?
As long as I start early, I will feel calm. Unless something serious happens, it will be wonderful.

If the weather is nice, I will eat in the garden.
As long as I leave early, I will arrive on time.
In case of a problem, I always prepare a backup.
Unless something serious happens, the plan will work.
If you prepare for the consequence, you will never panic.

I want to tell you about a trip I am planning, and how I am getting ready for it. If everything goes well, I will travel to the mountains next month. But I never travel without a plan. If the weather is bad, I will not drive the small roads; I will take the main highway instead. I always think about every situation before I go. As long as I leave early in the morning, I will arrive before dark, and that makes me feel safe. I also prepare a backup, just in case. If my phone runs out of battery, I will still have a paper map in my bag. Unless something serious happens, nothing will stop this trip. My wife always laughs at me, because I think about every consequence. But I tell her, if you prepare well, you will enjoy the trip much more. A good plan is the best way to relax.

Let me tell you how I am preparing for a small dinner with my friends this weekend. I love them, but I always plan everything, because I do not like surprises. If they all come, I will need a bigger table, so I have already asked my neighbor for hers. If the weather is nice, we will eat in the garden; otherwise, we will stay inside. I have a backup plan for the food too. In case someone does not eat meat, I will prepare a vegetable dish as well. As long as I start cooking early, I will not feel rushed, and I can enjoy the evening. I always think about every situation, because I know one small problem can change everything. Unless something goes really wrong, this dinner will be wonderful. My granddaughter says I worry too much, but I tell her, if you prepare for the consequence, you will never panic.

First, decide how many people will come, because the whole plan depends on it. Then, if the weather is nice, choose the garden; otherwise, prepare the living room. Next, in case someone does not eat meat, cook a vegetable dish as a backup. After that, as long as you start early, you will have time to relax before they arrive. Finally, think about every consequence, so unless something serious happens, nothing will surprise you.
